commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the intentional lifecycle timing change and normal merge gating.; Cleared: The diff only changes harness scheduling and focused tests; it does not touch dependencies, CI, secrets, auth, packaging, or code download/execution paths. (sha=cdbb70498204a767ceedeb6bbd3078f5837f63b7)
commands.review_summary:
This PR detaches Skill Research auto-capture from awaited agent_end side effects while preserving awaited plugin hook settlement and adding harness regression coverage.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +26. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows awaitAgentEndSideEffects awaits Skill Research auto-capture before plugin hook settlement, and the CLI caller uses that awaited helper for one-shot local runs; I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the intentional lifecycle timing change and normal merge gating.

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): align agent_end hook timeout with max reviewer timeout
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` placed Skill Research auto-capture on the awaited `agent_end` settlement path, so delayed capture work could delay routine session close.
- Root cause: `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` awaited `runCoreAgentEndSideEffects`, and that helper awaited `runSkillResearchAutoCapture` before plugin hook settlement.
- Fix classification: Root cause fix.

Why does this matter now?

- Why it matters / User impact: users closing routine local CLI sessions should not wait on Skill Research capture work before final output/session completion can settle.
- The existing PR had a current RF-001 review finding asking the author to account for the local session-close boundary change.

What is the intended outcome?

- Skill Research auto-capture starts opportunistically and may complete after final output.
- Plugin `agent_end` hooks keep their previous caller-sensitive behavior: awaited callers still await the plugin hook path.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the scheduling invariant is corrected at the harness side-effect boundary instead of raising timeouts, adding fallbacks, or masking slow capture work.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Scope boundary: only `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts` and `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts` changed.
- Out of scope: timeout budget changes, old extension restoration, public config/schema/protocol changes, storage/data format changes, model transcript changes, Desktop/Mantis/private CI/external channel coverage, and third-party account flows.

What does success look like?

- Focused harness tests pass on the rebased HEAD.
- A real Gateway agent turn returns final output before delayed Skill Research auto-capture writes its proposal.
- Capture failures remain caught/logged, and awaited plugin hooks are still invoked through the awaited hook helper.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The lifecycle boundary in `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts`.
- The focused regression that keeps capture pending while awaited side effects resolve.
- RF-001 below: this PR intentionally changes when Skill Research proposal capture may finish, while preserving plugin hook await behavior.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #87352

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87504

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

This is an existing PR repair. ClawSweeper review reported RF-001 against #87504, asking the author to account for the intentionally changed local session-close boundary.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Routine local CLI/session-close paths should not wait on delayed Skill Research auto-capture work before returning final agent output; plugin `agent_end` hooks should remain awaited for awaited callers.
- Real environment tested: Linux OpenClaw worktree at `cdbb70498204a767ceedeb6bbd3078f5837f63b7`; real `openclaw gateway run` plus `openclaw gateway call agent --expect-final`; isolated HOME/state/workspace; OpenAI-compatible `/v1/responses` endpoint; provider `openai`, model `gpt-5.5`; Skill Workshop autonomous capture enabled; 8000 existing workshop proposals preloaded outside the active workspace to delay capture.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm test -- src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts`
  - `HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node <real-gateway-delayed-autocapture-proof.mjs>`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  Validation `vitest-agent-end-side-effects` passed with exit_code=0 in 13806ms.

  $ node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  3 passed (3)
     Duration  887ms

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.22s
  ```

  ```text
  Validation `real-gateway-delayed-autocapture` passed with exit_code=0 in 100405ms.

  [2026-06-08T03:12:11.321Z] configured OpenAI-compatible model, gateway auth none, and skills.workshop.autonomous.enabled=true
  [2026-06-08T03:12:17.129Z] prepopulated 8000 workshop proposals outside active workspace
  [2026-06-08T03:12:24.986Z] github-pr-workflow proposals before agent run: 0
  [2026-06-08T03:12:42.402Z] END gateway-agent: exit=0
  [2026-06-08T03:12:42.433Z] gateway agent final returned in 17418ms; github-pr-workflow proposals immediately after final: 0
  [2026-06-08T03:12:58.785Z] github-pr-workflow proposal observed after final wait: count=1 elapsedMs=16351
  2026-06-08T11:12:42.279+08:00 OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_87504_GATEWAY
  2026-06-08T11:12:58.583+08:00 [skills/research] skill research auto-capture queued workshop proposal github-pr-workflow
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The harness regression proves `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` resolves while a Skill Research auto-capture promise is still pending, and still invokes the awaited plugin hook path. The real Gateway run returned the final agent response first; only after that did detached Skill Research auto-capture write the pending `github-pr-workflow` proposal.
- What was not tested: External channel, Desktop, Mantis, private CI, third-party account flows, and real provider account billing/auth flows were not exercised.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses an isolated local Gateway and an OpenAI-compatible local `/v1/responses` endpoint so the real Gateway/agent/session/autocapture path runs without external account credentials. This validates lifecycle scheduling and delayed capture completion, not provider network behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fix, the awaited helper shape put Skill Research auto-capture on the awaited session-close path. The regression keeps capture unresolved to cover that boundary.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts`
- `HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD) node <real-gateway-delayed-autocapture-proof.mjs>`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts` coverage showing `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` does not wait for pending Skill Research auto-capture.
- Kept coverage that fire-and-forget dispatch starts plugin hooks without waiting for capture.
- Kept coverage that capture rejection is caught/logged while awaited plugin hook execution still happens.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The previous awaited helper shape placed Skill Research auto-capture on the awaited session-close path. That meant delayed capture work could extend routine local session close before plugin hooks settled.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added and passed on the current rebased HEAD.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Skill Research proposal capture may complete after final output instead of being part of awaited `agent_end` settlement.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Highest-risk area: agent lifecycle scheduling and session-state timing.
- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: session-state`, and `merge-risk: compatibility`.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: current `origin/main` removed the old `extensions/skill-workshop` runtime. The active source of truth is the agent harness side-effect runner calling `runSkillResearchAutoCapture` from `src/skills/research/autocapture.ts`; plugin hook completion remains owned by `awaitAgentHarnessAgentEndHook`.
- Architecture boundary: this change affects when optional Skill Research proposal capture completes, not the model-consumed transcript source of truth, public config, schema, protocol, storage format, or plugin SDK contract.
- Related open PR scan: this is an existing PR repair for #87504, not a new same-contract competing PR; the current actionable review finding is RF-001.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Risk explanation: Skill Research auto-capture now starts detached, while awaited callers still await `awaitAgentHarnessAgentEndHook`; the session-state risk is limited to capture completion timing.
- Why acceptable: the diff is two files, preserves caught/logged capture failures, avoids timeout budget changes, and includes focused regression plus real Gateway proof.
- Patch quality notes: the `.catch` does not add a new silent fallback; it preserves the previous error-containment behavior from `try/catch` while moving it onto the detached promise so failures remain logged without blocking session close.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer-ready confidence: 90/100.
- Next action: author update is ready for local submit; then remote CI and maintainer/ClawSweeper review should evaluate RF-001 handling.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Author: no known remaining local code or proof blocker after the focused regression and real Gateway proof above.
- Maintainer/ClawSweeper: review RF-001 handling and decide whether the intentional boundary is acceptable.
- CI: remote checks need to run on the updated branch after submit.
- External proof: Desktop, Mantis, external channel, private CI, and third-party account flows remain out of scope for this harness-only repair.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 `[P1]`: Addressed as an intentional boundary change, not an accidental regression. Skill Research proposal capture is now opportunistic and may complete after final output; plugin `agent_end` hooks remain awaited for awaited callers. The focused regression proves the awaited side-effect function resolves without resolving pending capture, and the real Gateway proof shows final output returns before delayed capture writes the `github-pr-workflow` proposal.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
